key_highlights: U.S. added 115,000 jobs in April, boosting market sentiment despite Iran war concerns.
S&P 500 and Nasdaq posted 6th consecutive weekly win, driven by chip stock rally (Micron up ~38% on week).
ECB President Lagarde highlights dilemma between acting too early or too late on policy.
Iran war is rapidly draining global oil buffer, with DOJ probing suspicious oil futures trades.
Gold and silver prices heading for weekly gains; gold output in China falls as investor demand for bars/coins jumps.
Utilities sector sees multiple high-growth picks amid nuclear energy push and data center opportunities.
Crypto industry holds lap-dance party in bear market; Bitcoin and Ethereum prices stable after jobs report.
China's energy imports plunge as Hormuz shipments choked; IMF approves $1.32 billion tranche for Pakistan.
Rocket Lab stock jumps on biggest-ever launch contract; Intel stock achieves never-before-seen feat.
Goldman Sachs sees Fed cuts delayed to December, March on persistent inflation.

sectors.trend: Strong rally led by memory chip stocks (Micron +38% on week) and AI-related plays (Taiwan Semi, Arista Networks). Intel hits all-time high on Apple enthusiasm. Rocket Lab surges on record launch contract.
sectors.implications: Continued AI and data center demand driving semiconductor outperformance. Investors should monitor valuation risks but remain overweight on secular growth themes.

sectors.trend: High-growth utility stocks (PPL, BKH, CEG, CMS, DTE, PCG, FE, SR, PEG) are in focus due to nuclear energy push, data center opportunities, and Microsoft deal. Iran war is draining oil buffer, causing energy import plunge for China and fertilizer crisis in Africa.
sectors.implications: Utilities offer defensive growth with AI/data center tailwinds. Energy sector faces geopolitical risk premium; oil prices likely to remain elevated. Investors should consider utility exposure for stability and energy for tactical trades.

sectors.trend: Kraft Heinz beats Q1 expectations, rallying. Warby Parker stock soars. Expedia and Airbnb active in acquisitions. Blind box trend gaining traction among big brands. Lidl vs Aldi discount war wooing middle class.
sectors.implications: Consumer staples show resilience; discretionary travel recovering. Blind box trend could boost toy/retail innovation. Discount retailers benefit from inflation-conscious consumers.

sectors.trend: Goldman Sachs expected to go higher (Cramer). BofA issues blunt call on Planet Fitness. HELOC/home equity rates rise; equity-rich homeowners fall. Hong Kong home market maintains upward momentum. Safepoint (insurer) and Quantinuum (Honeywell-backed) file for IPOs.
sectors.implications: Financials benefit from higher rates and deal activity. Real estate faces affordability headwinds but selective markets (Hong Kong) remain strong. IPO market showing signs of revival.

sectors.trend: Privia Health Group reports Q1 results. Charles River Laboratories Q1 earnings call. Thermo Fisher seen as buy at current level (Cramer). Health officials downplay pandemic risk from cruise hantavirus outbreak.
sectors.implications: Healthcare remains defensive with selective growth in services and tools. Hantavirus risk is low but monitoring is warranted. Thermo Fisher attractive for long-term investors.

sectors.trend: Crypto industry throws lap-dance party in bear market. Bitcoin and Ethereum prices stable after strong jobs report. Stablecoin adoption discussed on Wall Street Week.
sectors.implications: Crypto market sentiment remains mixed; bear market persists but institutional adoption (stablecoins) continues. Investors should remain cautious but watch for regulatory clarity.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk: Iran war draining oil buffer, Hormuz clashes, China energy imports plunge, fertilizer crisis in Africa.
Monetary policy divergence: ECB torn on timing; Fed cuts delayed to December (Goldman); Colombia inflation ticks up.
AI and data center boom driving semiconductor and utility demand.
Labor market resilience: 115K jobs added in April despite Iran war impact.
Inflation persistence: US consumer unnerved; Goldman sees March 2027 for next cut.
China economic challenges: Gold output falls, local government debt defusion efforts, energy imports plunge.
IPO market revival: Safepoint, Quantinuum file for IPOs.
Regulatory scrutiny: DOJ probing suspicious oil trades; FDA leadership turmoil.
Consumer behavior shift: Blind box trend, discount retail wars, home equity decline.
Corporate earnings bonanza: Record stock run fueled by earnings beats (Kraft Heinz, Rocket Lab, etc.).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Iran War Escalation and Oil Supply Disruption
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy stocks (XOM, CVX) and commodities (gold, silver). Hedge with oil futures or ETFs. Reduce exposure to sectors highly sensitive to oil prices (airlines, shipping).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Persistent Inflation and Delayed Fed Rate Cuts
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor short-duration bonds, TIPS, and value stocks. Avoid long-duration fixed income. Consider floating-rate notes and inflation-protected securities.
Diversify geographically; increase exposure to Asia ex-China and domestic US. Monitor Trump-Xi summit outcomes. Hold cash for opportunistic buying.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and Legal Risks (DOJ Probe, FDA Turmoil)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid stocks with high regulatory exposure (oil traders, prediction markets). Monitor FDA leadership changes for biotech/pharma impact. Maintain compliance-focused portfolio.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: China Economic Slowdown and Debt Risks
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to China-sensitive sectors (commodities, luxury goods). Favor domestic US and India markets. Monitor local government debt defusion progress.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer Weakness and Housing Market Softening
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid overvalued consumer discretionary; focus on staples and discount retailers. Monitor HELOC rates and equity-rich homeowner data. Consider defensive REITs.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy semiconductor and AI-related stocks on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: Chip stocks (Micron, Taiwan Semi, Arista) are on a tear driven by AI demand. Intel's new feat and Rocket Lab's contract signal sustained growth. Use dips to add positions.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate high-growth utility stocks with data center/nuclear exposure
opportunities.rationale: Utilities like PPL, BKH, CEG, CMS, DTE, PCG, FE, SR, PEG offer defensive growth with AI/data center tailwinds and nuclear energy push. Microsoft deal strengthens BKH.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in gold and silver as geopolitical hedges
opportunities.rationale: Gold and silver heading for weekly gains; China gold output falls while investor demand jumps. Iran war and inflation fears support precious metals. Allocate 5-10% of portfolio.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy beaten-down consumer staples like Kraft Heinz on earnings momentum
opportunities.rationale: Kraft Heinz beat Q1 expectations and rallied. Cramer calls it cheap. Defensive positioning with inflation resilience. Attractive dividend yield.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider IPO participation in Safepoint and Quantinuum
opportunities.rationale: IPO market reviving with insurance (Safepoint) and quantum computing (Quantinuum). Early-stage growth potential. Allocate small portion for high risk/reward.

defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical risks (Iran war, Hormuz), delayed Fed cuts, and inflation persistence warrant higher cash buffer for opportunistic buying during volatility.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge oil price exposure with put options or inverse ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Iran war draining oil buffer; DOJ probing suspicious trades. Oil prices could spike further, hurting airlines and shipping. Use hedges to protect portfolio.
China energy imports plunge, local government debt risks, and gold output decline. Avoid luxury goods, commodities, and tech hardware with China supply chains.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Rotate from long-duration bonds to short-duration TIPS
defensive_moves.rationale: Goldman sees Fed cuts delayed to December/March. Inflation persists. Short-duration TIPS provide inflation protection with lower interest rate risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor and avoid stocks with regulatory/legal overhang
defensive_moves.rationale: DOJ probing oil trades and prediction markets; FDA leadership turmoil. Avoid companies under investigation or with heavy regulatory exposure.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets likely to remain volatile due to Iran war escalation, oil supply disruptions, and delayed Fed cuts. However, strong earnings season and AI-driven tech rally provide support. S&P 500 could test new highs but faces headwinds from inflation and geopolitical risks. Expect sector rotation into utilities, energy, and defensive consumer staples. Gold and silver to remain bid. Crypto may stay range-bound.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Positive if Iran conflict de-escalates and Fed begins cutting by year-end. AI and data center capex cycle supports tech and utilities. China's economic challenges and local government debt remain structural drags. Global trade disruptions (Hormuz) could reshape energy flows. Inflation may stay above target, keeping rates higher for longer. IPO market revival signals risk appetite returning. Long-term investors should focus on quality growth at reasonable prices.
key_catalysts: Iran war ceasefire or escalation developments
Trump-Xi summit outcomes (trade, tariffs, geopolitical cooperation)
Fed meeting minutes and dot plot (next meeting June 2026)
US CPI and PCE inflation data (May/June releases)
China stimulus measures and local government debt resolution
Semiconductor earnings (Nvidia, AMD, Micron next quarters)
Oil inventory data and strategic petroleum reserve releases
US presidential election campaign developments (2026 midterms?)
watch_list: Iran war: Hormuz strait shipping disruptions, oil prices (WTI, Brent)
DOJ investigation outcomes on oil futures trades
Inflation indicators: CPI, PCE, producer prices
ECB Lagarde comments on rate path
Bitcoin ETF flows and regulatory news
Utility sector earnings and data center demand updates
Home equity and mortgage rate trends
